name: Leominster Employees
description_short: NCUA-insured federal credit union serving City of Leominster employees and families since 1936, offering savings accounts, CDs, mortgages, and loans.
description_long: Leominster Employees Federal Credit Union was established in 1936 and operates as a federally chartered, NCUA-insured credit union headquartered in Leominster, Massachusetts. With approximately $27.61 million in assets, the institution maintains a focused membership model restricted to employees of the City of Leominster and their immediate family members. This membership restriction allows the credit union to serve a defined community with personalized banking relationships. The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking products including savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) with multiple term options, mortgage lending for both purchase and refinance, home equity lines of credit (HELOC), auto loans, personal loans, and interest-bearing checking accounts. Their current deposit rates include 4.20% APY on savings and CD rates ranging from 4.00% to 4.59% depending on term length. Mortgage products are available at competitive rates, with 30-year fixed mortgages quoted at 4.91% APR. What distinguishes Leominster Employees is its tight community focus and membership affiliation with municipal employees, which creates a stable member base and allows for personalized service. As a credit union rather than a commercial bank, it operates on a not-for-profit basis, potentially offering better rates and terms to members compared to traditional banks. The institution has maintained consistent operations for nearly 90 years, suggesting organizational stability and community trust. The primary limitation is the restricted field of membership—only City of Leominster employees and their families qualify for accounts. Additionally, current rate and loan information on third-party comparison sites is incomplete or outdated, requiring direct contact with the credit union for accurate product details. The institution's smaller asset base ($27.61 million) compared to larger regional credit unions may limit product sophistication or digital banking capabilities.
services: Savings accounts with interest bearing options
Certificates of deposit (CDs) with multiple term lengths (1, 2, 5-year terms)
Loan products for City of Leominster employee members
pros: NCUA federal insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000
Competitive CD rates up to 4.59% APY on 1-year terms as of April 2026
Mortgage rates at 4.91% (30-year) and 4.39% (15-year) competitive with market
Not-for-profit credit union structure may offer better rates than commercial banks
Long operational history since 1936 indicates institutional stability
Multiple loan products including auto, personal, and home equity options
cons: Membership restricted to City of Leominster employees and families only—no open membership
Rate and loan product information is incomplete or outdated on public comparison sites, requiring direct inquiry
Small asset base ($27.61 million) may limit product variety, digital banking features, or service hours compared to larger institutions
Limited branch network with only headquarters location confirmed on website
No online account opening or account management details provided
best_for: City of Leominster municipal employees seeking personalized community banking with competitive rates
Families of Leominster city workers who want to consolidate banking with a trusted local institution
Borrowers seeking mortgage or home equity products with a credit union focused on local customer service
Members prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and not-for-profit banking principles
diagnosis: Leominster Employees Federal Credit Union is best suited for current and former employees of the City of Leominster and their families seeking relationship banking with competitive rates backed by federal insurance. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is strictly limited to this specific employee group, making it inaccessible to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
